key_points: Cells are the basic structural and functional units of life
Cell theory states that all organisms are made of cells and cells arise from pre-existing cells
Cells may be unicellular or multicellular depending on the organism
The plasma membrane is selectively permeable and regulates movement of substances
Diffusion and osmosis help transport materials across the cell membrane
The cell wall in plant cells provides rigidity, protection and structural support
The nucleus contains chromosomes which carry genetic information in the form of DNA
Endoplasmic reticulum helps in synthesis and transport of proteins and lipids
Golgi apparatus modifies, packages and transports materials within the cell
Mitochondria perform cellular respiration and produce ATP for energy
Lysosomes contain digestive enzymes for intracellular digestion
Plastids such as chloroplasts help in photosynthesis in plant cells
Vacuoles maintain turgidity and store water, nutrients and wastes
Cell division occurs through mitosis and meiosis
Mitosis produces identical cells while meiosis produces gametes with half chromosome number
examples: Storage organelle in plant cells: Vacuole
formulas: Diffusion → Movement of particles from higher concentration to lower concentration
Osmosis → Movement of water through a semi-permeable membrane
Mitosis → One parent cell forms two identical daughter cells
Meiosis → One parent cell forms four daughter cells with half chromosome number
exam_tips: Remember the three statements of cell theory
Understand the difference between plant and animal cells
Draw neat labelled diagrams of plant cell and animal cell
Remember the functions of important organelles like mitochondria, nucleus and ribosomes
Understand diffusion and osmosis with examples
